2026-03-24T19:16:27.7650293Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-24T19:16:18.585406366Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\nThank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.\n\n**Summary:** The issue reports that using `ClientSecretCredential` (service principal) with `Azure.AI.Projects` 2.0.0-beta.2 returns an empty response from `GetOutputText()`, while `DefaultAzureCredential` works correctly. The author suspects the issue may be a missing role assignment on the service principal, but is also noting the SDK provides no error/exception to help diagnose the problem.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Analysis Details\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n### Issue Classification\n- **Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects` 2.0.0-beta.2\n- **Category:** `Client` — this is a data-plane SDK (non-management)\n- **Service:** `AI Projects` — maps to `/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects`\n- **Label confidence:** High — the package is explicitly named, the category (Client) and service (AI Projects) are unambiguous\n\n### Possible Root Causes\n\n1. **Missing RBAC role for service principal:** The assigned roles (`Reader`, `Azure AI User`, `Cognitive Services User`, `Cognitive Services OpenAI User`) may be insufficient for the AI Foundry Responses API with a service principal. Some operations may require additional roles such as **Azure AI Developer** or **Cognitive Services OpenAI Contributor**.\n\n2. **Silent failure / missing error propagation in the SDK:** The `GetOutputText()` method returning an empty string instead of throwing an exception when authentication fails or the request is silently rejected is a legitimate SDK behavior concern. There should ideally be an error surface for this scenario.\n\n3. **Credential scope mismatch:** `ClientSecretCredential` and `DefaultAzureCredential` may request different token scopes or audiences. The service may be silently returning an empty/filtered response for tokens that lack the correct scope.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🛠️ Suggested Debugging Steps\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Verify the credential is acquiring a token** by wrapping it:\n   ```csharp\n   var credential = new ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecret);\n   var token = await credential.GetTokenAsync(new TokenRequestContext(new[] { \"https://ai.azure.com/.default\" }));\n   Console.WriteLine(\"Token acquired: \" + token.Token[..20] + \"...\");\n   ```\n\n2. **Check if the `ResponseResult` has additional properties** beyond `GetOutputText()` — look for `Status`, error fields, or inspect the raw response if available.\n\n3. **Try adding the `Azure AI Developer` role** to your service principal, as `Azure AI User` alone may not grant permission for Responses API calls.\n\n4. **Enable HTTP logging** to inspect the raw requests/responses:\n   ```csharp\n   using Azure.Core.Diagnostics;\n   using var listener = AzureEventSourceListener.CreateConsoleLogger(EventLevel.Verbose);\n   ```\n\n5. **Compare token claims** between the `DefaultAzureCredential` token and the `ClientSecretCredential` token using https://jwt.ms to identify scope or role differences.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📚 Related Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [Azure AI Projects SDK README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/README.md)\n- [Azure AI Foundry RBAC documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/rbac-ai-foundry)\n- [Azure.Identity ClientSecretCredential docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.identity.clientsecretcredential)\n- [Azure SDK logging guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/logging)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Similar Issues\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nNo exact duplicates found for this specific `ClientSecretCredential` + `Azure.AI.Projects` behavior. Issue [#52283](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/52283) is a related question about `Azure.AI.Projects` chat completion APIs but is a distinct concern.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":"57414"},"name":"add_comment"}}}

2026-03-24T19:16:27.8076791Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-03-24T19:16:27.8077070Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-03-24T19:16:27.8077217Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-03-24T19:16:27.8077435Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-03-24T19:16:27.8077778Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-03-24T19:16:27.8077888Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
2026-03-24T19:16:27.8078026Z [36;1m  awf logs summary | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"[0m
2026-03-24T19:16:27.8078101Z [36;1melse[0m
